step_by_step_thinking:
The tingling sensation is likely due to peripheral neuropathy, which can occur as an adverse effect from certain antituberculosis drugs like ethambutol or pyrazinamide. Among the listed vitamins, only pyridoxine (Vitamin B6) has been shown to have some benefit in treating this type of neurotoxicity by reducing levels of its toxic metabolite, especially when taken with ethambulot. Therefore:

Answer C: Pyridoxine
